title: IR Motion Alarm System
subtitle: detect motion, trigger alarm!
overview: In this session, students learn how an IR motion sensor detects nearby objects and triggers a buzzer alarm using Arduino UNO. Students understand sensors, automation, and basic security systems through hands-on activities.

images._note: Add real classroom photos, Arduino setup photos, IR sensor demos, buzzer activity, and student experiments.

activity.title: Build an IR Motion Alarm System
steps: Place the IR sensor on the breadboard
Connect OUT pin of IR sensor to digital pin 2
Connect buzzer positive pin to digital pin 8
Connect buzzer negative pin to GND
Move hand in front of sensor
Buzzer alarm activates when motion is detected!

quiz.explanation: IR stands for Infrared — a type of invisible light used in sensors.

quiz.q: What is the main function of an IR sensor?
quiz.explanation: IR sensors detect objects by sending and receiving infrared light.

quiz.q: Which component produces the alarm sound?
quiz.explanation: The buzzer creates sound when motion is detected.

quiz.q: Which board is used as the brain of the project?
quiz.explanation: Arduino UNO controls the entire motion alarm system.

quiz.q: What happens when motion is detected?
quiz.explanation: The buzzer activates to alert users when motion is detected.

quiz.q: Which pin of the IR sensor sends signal to Arduino?
quiz.explanation: The OUT pin sends detection signals to Arduino.

quiz.q: What type of signal does the IR sensor give?
quiz.explanation: The IR sensor provides digital HIGH/LOW signals.

quiz.q: Which component helps connect parts without soldering?
quiz.explanation: Breadboards allow easy circuit building and testing.

quiz.q: What is the use of jumper wires?
quiz.explanation: Jumper wires connect electronic components together.

quiz.q: Where are IR motion alarms commonly used?
quiz.explanation: IR motion alarms are widely used in security and automation systems.
